_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot, batch CA freshmen + Chu)
_meta.note: 5-record CA federal House batch: vacant-ca-14 (candidacy-currency refresh — Swalwell resigned 2026-04-14, special primary 2026-06-16), george-whitesides (CA-27 freshman D, cross-party Laken-Riley YEA + AIPAC 'model' Iran posture), judy-chu (CA-28 16-yr incumbent + Pro-Choice Caucus chair, WHPA lead author), luz-rivas (CA-29 freshman D, AIPAC trip cross-cut), laura-friedman (CA-30 freshman D, prior CA Assembly AB 2223/AB 1955/AB 1594 author). Evidence-first: every FALSE cites a roll call, sponsorship, endorsement, or named rating; null where no evidence; never inferred from party.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-05 — Eric Swalwell (D) resigned April 14, 2026 amid sexual-misconduct allegations and an announced House Ethics investigation. Gov. Newsom signed a special-election proclamation the same day. Special primary 2026-06-16; runoff (if needed) 2026-08-18. No successor seated as of this update; record kept roster_verified with null scores until a winner is certified.

vacant-ca-14.notes_set: VACANT (CA-14) — Eric Swalwell (D) resigned April 14, 2026 (sexual-misconduct allegations + House Ethics probe + DA investigation; had earlier suspended his CA-gubernatorial campaign 2026-04-12). Gov. Newsom proclamation same day set Special Primary 2026-06-16 and Special Runoff (only if no majority) 2026-08-18. As of 2026-06-05, 11 candidates on the special-primary ballot — D: Aisha Wahab (State Sen.), Melissa Hernandez (fmr Dublin Mayor), Rakhi Israni, Alisha Cordes, Sheriene Ridenour, Jot Thiara; R: Wendy Huang, Dena Maldonado, Tom Wong, Jack Wu; Ind: Victor Zevallos. Seat remains vacant; record kept roster_verified with null scores pending winner. The June 2 regular primary (full Jan-2027 term) is a separate election from the June 16 special.

profile.religion: Episcopalian (per Wikipedia category 'Episcopalians from California'; no specific parish cited)
profile.background: U.S. Representative CA-27 (Santa Clarita / Antelope Valley), since Jan 2025. Defeated GOP incumbent Mike Garcia in 2024. Princeton (BA) + King's College Cambridge (MPhil); ED of National Space Society; NASA Chief of Staff under Obama (2009-10); CEO Virgin Galactic (2010-21). Sits on House Armed Services Committee. Wife Loretta Hidalgo Whitesides; 2 children.

george-whitesides.notes_set: U.S. Rep CA-27 (D), since Jan 2025 — freshman, frontline-D in a swing district. [2026-06 national autopilot, federal tier] Reliably progressive on life/marriage/guns: cosponsor H.R. 12 Women's Health Protection Act (codify Roe) + H.R. 4888 Reproductive Rights Are Human Rights Act; cosponsor H.R. 15 Equality Act (adds SOGI to Civil Rights Act, overrides RFRA); Planned Parenthood Action Fund + Reproductive Freedom for All + EMILY's List endorsements; Giffords PAC endorsement ($1M+ supporting; explicit pro-red-flag, pro-AWB, pro-UBC commitments). TWO HARD CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: (1) Voted YEA on H.R. 29 Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025) — one of only ~48 House Dems crossing the caucus for mandatory ICE detention of illegal aliens charged with theft/violent crime, and his campaign policy page openly backs 'physical infrastructure where appropriate' + more Border Patrol + tech; (2) AIPAC publicly held up his June 2025 Iran statement ('cannot be allowed to develop an operational nuclear weapon') as a 'model' for other Democrats — also endorsed by Democratic Majority for Israel + Jewish Democratic Council of America (HASC seat reinforces a hawkish posture atypical of a freshman D). Heritage Action 0% lifetime. Several dimensions left null absent cited roll calls.

0.note: Cosponsor H.R. 12 Women's Health Protection Act (codify Roe — opposes conception/personhood premise); endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund as a 'reproductive rights champion.'

1.note: Cosponsor H.R. 4888 Reproductive Rights Are Human Rights Act — affirmatively advocates expansion, not abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund + Reproductive Freedom for All + EMILY's List; accepted their backing.

0.note: Cosponsor H.R. 15 Equality Act of 2025 — adds sexual orientation/gender identity to Civil Rights Act, rejects one-man/one-woman premise.

1.note: Equality Act cosponsorship codifies same-sex unions as protected status.

2.note: Equality Act expressly adds 'gender identity' as protected class — rejects biological-sex-as-immutable framing.

4.note: Equality Act mandates LGBTQ protections across employment/education/public accommodations.

0.note: Endorsed by California Teachers Association + California Federation of Teachers — public-school-funding alignment, no universal school choice support.

1.note: Equality Act cosponsorship overrides RFRA — eliminates conscience exemptions for Christian businesses, adoption agencies, medical professionals.

2.note: Voted YEA on H.R. 29 Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025) — one of ~48 House Dem crossovers; mandates federal ICE detention for illegal aliens charged with theft/violent crime, preempting sanctuary-jurisdiction releases.

0.note: Giffords PAC endorsement; supports universal background checks + permit-to-purchase, rejects constitutional carry.

1.note: Giffords endorsement explicitly commits to supporting red-flag laws ('disarming those who pose a threat'), an assault-weapons ban, and CVI funding.

3.note: Endorsed by Democratic Majority for Israel + Jewish Democratic Council of America; AIPAC publicly held up his Iran statement as a 'model' for other Democrats — pro-foreign-lobby alignment.

profile.religion: Unitarian Universalist (since mid-1980s); frames religion as social-justice action
profile.background: U.S. Representative CA-28 (San Gabriel Valley) since 2009 special election; first Chinese-American woman elected to Congress. BA Math UCLA (1974); PhD Psychology, California School of Professional Psychology (1979). 20-year LA Community College District psychology professor (13 at East LA College). Garvey School Board → Monterey Park Mayor → CA State Assembly → CA Board of Equalization → U.S. House. Chair, Congressional Asian Pacific American Caucus (CAPAC). Chair, Pro-Choice Caucus Contraception/Family Planning/Education Task Force.

judy-chu.notes_set: U.S. Rep CA-28 (D), since 2009 — 16-yr incumbent, signature pro-choice voice. [2026-06 national autopilot, federal tier] Lead author of the Women's Health Protection Act (codify Roe — reintroduced 2021/2022/2023/2025); voted NO on H.R. 26 Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Jan 11, 2023) with cited floor remarks; original lead/cosponsor of Respect for Marriage Act; lead sponsor PRIDE Act 2023/2025 (tax-equity for same-sex couples) on Windsor anniversary; voted NO on H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights Act (Mar 24, 2023); public opposition to SAVE Act (citizenship-verified voting); voted NO on H.R. 29 Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025); voted to terminate Trump's border-emergency declaration (defund wall); statement opposing Trump's birthright-citizenship EO; original cosponsor H.R. 3115 Assault Weapons Ban of 2023; voted YES on Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (red-flag funding). Heritage Action lifetime 9%. One intra-Dem deviation: voted NO on H.R. 8034 (Israel offensive-aid supplemental, April 2024) — a progressive left-flank break, not a conservative crossover. Several dimensions left null absent cited roll calls.

0.note: Lead author Women's Health Protection Act (codify Roe — reintroduced 2021/2022/2023/2025); affirmatively rejects life-at-conception premise.

1.note: Voted NO on H.R. 26 Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Jan 11, 2023); cited floor remarks framing the bill as an attack on 'a woman's right.'

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund + EMILY's List.

0.note: Original lead/cosponsor of Respect for Marriage Act (codifies same-sex marriage, repeals DOMA).

1.note: Lead sponsor PRIDE Act (tax-equity for same-sex couples), introduced on Windsor decision anniversary.

4.note: Member, Congressional Equality Caucus; affirmative LGBTQ-policy advocate.

1.note: Voted NO on H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights Act (Mar 24, 2023) — opposed parental notification/consent framework.
As CAPAC Chair publicly denounced the SAVE Act (citizenship-verified voter registration) as 'voter suppression.'

0.note: Voted to terminate Trump's southern-border emergency declaration (defunded wall construction).

1.note: Voted NO on H.R. 29 Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025) — opposed mandatory ICE detention of illegal aliens charged with theft/violent crime.

4.note: Public statement opposing Trump's birthright-citizenship executive order, citing 14th Amendment.

1.note: Original cosponsor H.R. 3115 Assault Weapons Ban of 2023; voted YES on Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (red-flag funding + enhanced under-21 checks + boyfriend-loophole close).

profile.religion: Catholic (per Wikipedia; no specific parish cited; opposed state allowing religious institutions to build housing on owned land — negative-religious-autonomy land-use posture)
profile.background: U.S. Representative CA-29 (East San Fernando Valley) since Jan 2025 (succeeded retiring Tony Cárdenas). Born 1974 Los Angeles; daughter of Mexican immigrants; first Latina representing CA-29. CA Assembly (AD-39, then AD-43) 2018-2024. BS Electrical Engineering MIT (1996); MEd Harvard Graduate School of Education. Former Motorola engineer; founder of DIY Girls (Pacoima STEM nonprofit). Member, Congressional Progressive Caucus.

luz-rivas.notes_set: U.S. Rep CA-29 (D), since Jan 2025 — freshman, progressive-caucus aligned. [2026-06 national autopilot, federal tier] First congressional vote was NO on H.R. 29 Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025); subsequently voted NO on three ICE funding bills per her own press releases. EMILY's List + Planned Parenthood Action Fund + Reproductive Freedom for All endorsements. As CA Assembly member supported the 2022-2024 abortion-expansion package (Repro Health Equity Fund, Practical Support Fund, blocking out-of-state lawsuits, expanding non-physician providers). Did not break from caucus on AB 1955 (2024 CA ban on school parental-notification of student gender identity) — Equality California endorsement. Giffords PAC endorsement with explicit AWB/UBC/dating-partner-firearm commitments. Voted NO on H.R. 22 SAVE Act (Apr 10, 2025) and NO on the FY26 reconciliation 'One Big Beautiful Bill' (cited ICE/detention funding). Heritage Action 0% session + lifetime. ONE CROSS-CUT DEVIATION (within her party, not a conservative crossover): accepted $55K+ from pro-Israel PACs in 2023-2024 and took an AIPAC-funded Israel trip Aug 9, 2025 with Reps. Aguilar and Cisneros — hawkish foreign-policy posture atypical of CPC members. Several dimensions left null absent cited federal roll calls.

0.note: EMILY's List endorsement explicitly tied to 'pro-choice majority that will protect reproductive freedom'; CA Assembly record supported full 2022-2024 abortion-expansion package.

1.note: Planned Parenthood Action Fund + Reproductive Freedom for All endorsements — affirmatively advocates expansion, not abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List + Planned Parenthood Action Fund + Reproductive Freedom for All + PODER PAC.

0.note: Equality California endorsement — affirmative LGBTQ-policy advocate, rejects one-man/one-woman premise.

2.note: Did not break from caucus on AB 1955 (2024 CA ban on school staff notifying parents of student gender-identity change) — affirms trans-identity policy framework.

4.note: Equality California endorsement signals LGBTQ-policy promotion in public institutions.

1.note: Aligned with AB 1955 majority — opposes mandatory parental notification for gender-identity intervention.

2.note: AB 1955 entrenches SOGI/gender-ideology framework in K-12 by prohibiting parental-notification policies.

1.note: Voted NO on H.R. 22 SAVE Act (Apr 10, 2025, Roll Call 102) — opposed citizenship-verified voter registration.
First congressional vote was NO on H.R. 29 Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025) — opposed mandatory ICE detention of illegal aliens charged with theft/violent crime.
Giffords endorsement explicitly committed to assault-weapons ban + universal background checks + dating-partner firearm restrictions.

3.note: Accepted $55K+ from pro-Israel/AIPAC-aligned PACs 2023-2024; took AIPAC-funded Israel trip Aug 9, 2025 with Reps. Aguilar + Cisneros.

profile.religion: Jewish (active in Glendale Jewish community circles, ADL-aligned events); no public profession of Christ
profile.background: U.S. Representative CA-30 (Burbank/Glendale — Adam Schiff's former seat) since Jan 2025 (Schiff moved to U.S. Senate). Glendale City Council 2009-2016 (Mayor 2011-12 + 2014-15); CA State Assembly AD-43/AD-44 (2016-2024). BA Boston University 1988 (communications/film); ~20-yr career as film/TV producer before politics. EMILY's List + Planned Parenthood Action Fund + HRC + Giffords/Brady + AIPAC/Democratic Majority for Israel endorsements.

laura-friedman.notes_set: U.S. Rep CA-30 (D), since Jan 2025 — freshman, rich prior CA Assembly record (2016-2024). [2026-06 national autopilot, federal tier] Lead/sponsor of CA AB 2223 (2022) removing liability for pregnancy loss 'due to causes that occurred in utero'; YES on CA AB 1184 (2021, dependent-insurer confidentiality / parental-notification blocker); YES on CA SB 380 (assisted-suicide expansion limiting conscience protections); YES on CA AB 1955 (2024 school parental-notification ban); YES on CA AB 1594 (2022 Firearm Industry Responsibility Act, civil-liability exposure for gunmakers); lead author CA AB 2098 (2022 physician COVID 'misinformation' discipline law, later struck/repealed); lead author CA AB 1817 (2022 PFAS ban — ESG-aligned). Federal 119th: NO on H.R. 21 Born-Alive (Jan 23, 2025); NO on H.R. 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Jan 14, 2025); NO on H.R. 22 SAVE Act (Apr 10, 2025); NO on H.R. 29 Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025). AIPAC + Democratic Majority for Israel endorsements (hawkish for a CA progressive). No identified cross-party deviation toward Christian-conservative positions; closest deviation is intra-Dem (pro-Israel posture vs Squad). Several dimensions left null absent cited federal roll calls.

0.note: Lead sponsor / vote YES on CA AB 2223 (2022) removing civil/criminal liability for pregnancy loss 'due to causes that occurred in utero' — rejects conception/personhood framework.

1.note: Voted NO on H.R. 21 Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (Jan 23, 2025).

3.note: Voted YES on CA SB 380 (assisted-suicide expansion / End of Life Option Act amendments).

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List + Planned Parenthood Action Fund.

2.note: Voted NO on H.R. 28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (Jan 14, 2025) — opposes biological-sex-based athletic categories.

4.note: Human Rights Campaign 100% rating on CA Assembly equality scorecards; multiple Glendale Pride proclamations as Mayor.

1.note: Voted YES on CA AB 1955 (2024 SAFETY Act) banning school districts from requiring staff to notify parents of student gender-identity change.

2.note: Voted YES on CA AB 1078 (2023) limiting local school boards from removing books/curriculum framed as reflecting cultural/racial diversity.

1.note: Voted YES on CA SB 380 which limited conscience protections for some providers in the End of Life Option Act framework.
Voted NO on H.R. 22 SAVE Act (Apr 10, 2025) — opposed citizenship-verified voter registration.
Voted NO on H.R. 29 Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025) — opposed mandatory ICE detention of illegal aliens charged with theft/violent crime.

2.note: CA Assembly record consistently supported SB 54 sanctuary-state implementation bills.

1.note: Voted YES on CA AB 1594 (2022 Firearm Industry Responsibility Act) exposing gunmakers to civil liability.

3.note: AIPAC + Democratic Majority for Israel endorsements / PAC support (FEC filings).

0.note: Lead author CA AB 2098 (2022) creating physician discipline for COVID 'misinformation' — restricts informed-consent dialogue between physicians and patients on mandates.
